person.bio: Starting my journey as a Linux system engineer, evolving through roles as DevOps engineer and now being a cloud engineer, I have discovered my passion for automation and cloud technologies. With expertise in Cloud (Azure), Kubernetes (AKS, on-prem), IaC (Terraform, Ansible, Puppet), software development (Python), CI/CD (GitHub actions, FluxCD, ArgoCD) and monitoring (Prometheus, Icinga), I am always excited to dive into new innovations. I love sharing my knowledge and discussing challenging topics to support others in their development and success.

person.name: Jonah Kowall
person.bio: Jonah Kowall, computer scientist and open-source advocate. As a member of the founding team and technical leadership committee for OpenSearch a Linux Foundation project, Maintainer of Jaeger a CNCF graduated project for distributed tracing. Contributor to OpenTelemetry. A technical leader across startups and large enterprises specialized in operations, security, and performance. Led Gartner research on monitoring. Product leadership at AppDynamics, Cisco (post-acquisition), Kentik, Logz.io, Aiven, and is currently the Senior Vice President product and design at Paessler, the monitoring company.

person.name: Muhammad Adeel Arshad
person.bio: Serving as Cloud Solution Architect and Head of DevOps at tkxel, where I drive cloud-native adoption and infrastructure innovation. Having Solution Architect professional and DevOps professional certifications in AWS, Azure, and GCP. I specialize in Kubernetes orchestration, container security, CI/CD pipelines, and service mesh technologies across the CNCF landscape. A passionate community builder, I organize local cloud-native meetups and mentor aspiring engineers, committed to elevating Pakistan's presence in the global tech ecosystem.

person.name: Raham Shaik
person.bio: My Name is Raham, A passionate Cloud and DevOps Engineer and Trainer who loves to teach new trends and stuff to my students. Till now i've trained more than 10,000 Students and working Professionals to Achieve their dream job and in my free time i'm busy in making the content. As of now havent got any chance to participate on CNCF meetings but hoping soon. link to my article: https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/day-1-k8s-from-zero-1-raham-shaik-6odec

person.name: Sandeep V.
person.bio: Senior Full-Stack Software Engineer with over 10 years of experience specializing in cloud-native technologies like Kubernetes orchestration, container security, microservices migration, Java/Spring Boot backend development, Angular frontend applications, relational databases (PostgreSQL/MySQL), and AWS cloud infrastructure.

person.name: Shogo Shimahara
person.bio: Engineer based in Japan.  I have strong passion for learning cloud-native technologies. My current work involves advising usage and troubleshooting technical issues for managed Kubernetes and container-based services on Microsoft Azure.

person.name: Mark Wilson
person.bio: Mark is a DevOps Engineer working in FinTech in Melbourne, where he plays a co-organiser role at K8SUG - Australia's largest Kubernetes user group. With a background in software development spanning over 6+ years, Mark combines professional expertise with a lifelong passion for IT.

person.name: Ronak Padhya
person.bio: With hands-on experience across AWS, Azure, and GCP, I specialize in managing multiple Kubernetes clusters and building scalable, secure, and optimized platforms on Cloud. Passionate about Cloud, Kubernetes, and DevOps technologies, I am always eager to explore and work with the latest tools in the space. I have always focused on creating practical solutions that improve efficiency and spark innovation. Feel free to reach out to me on LinkedIn to discuss technology trends and insights.

person.name: Carlos Encarnacion
person.bio: Hello, my name is Carlos Encarnación. I am an engineer passionate about innovation and emerging technologies. Throughout my career, I have gained extensive knowledge and experience, and my greatest aspiration is to continue growing and exploring within this fascinating field. Embracing new challenges in this domain is, without a doubt, my passion and my preferred pursuit.

person.name: Cláudio Da Silva Pinheiro Júnior
person.bio: I am a DevOps and Site Reliability Engineer (SRE) with extensive experience in designing and maintaining scalable and secure infrastructure on Kubernetes, leveraging CNCF technologies such as ArgoCD, Crossplane, and Prometheus. I specialize in Infrastructure as Code (IaC), GitOps, and cloud-native solutions, working across platforms like AWS, Azure, GCP, and Oracle Cloud. My expertise extends to managing Kubernetes clusters (EKS, AKS, GKE, and more), automating deployment pipelines, and ensuring high availability and disaster recovery strategies.

person.name: Juan Carlos Coto Sanchez
person.bio: It all started about seven years ago when a professor in the first university class I ever took taught us about Linux. I still remember his words: "I should be teaching you other stuff according to the schedule, but instead, I will teach you something that is worth learning." Since then, it's been so much fun that I keep learning and learning. I have worked in a variety of technical roles providing support, technical consultancy, and automation. I have a passion for cloud-native technologies and experience in Kubernetes, containers, IaC, service mesh, cloud, and recently, CNAPP.

person.name: Rayaguru Satyanarayan Dash
person.bio: Meet Rayaguru, a seasoned database expert with over 18 years of hands-on experience across a spectrum of database technologies, for clients seeking migration, design, and performance tuning solutions.  He has empowered numerous clients to optimize their systems, ensuring seamless operations and enhanced efficiency. With a passion for unravelling complexities and a track record of delivering impactful solutions, Rayaguru continues to be a driving force in the realm of database consultancy.

person.name: Harsh Manvar
person.bio: I am a distinguished Docker Captain based in India, recognized for my expertise in containerization and cloud technologies. As a Cloud Innovator Champion, I'm committed to driving innovation and best practices in cloud computing. Additionally, i am a Top Kubernetes answerer worldwide on StackOverflow, demonstrating my dedication to fostering knowledge sharing within the global tech community.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: Becoming a CNCF ambassador is thrilling because it means actively shaping the future of cloud-native technologies, collaborating with industry leaders, and bridging gaps in the tech ecosystem. It's an opportunity to contribute to a global community that's driving innovation and setting standards for modern computing.

person.name: Jon Zeolla
person.bio: Jon Zeolla is the founder of Zenable, where they are revolutionizing corporate governance by integrating cloud-native security and compliance with artificial intelligence. Previously he co-founded Seiso where he received the 2021 Start-Up Innovator of the Year Award and currently serves as strategic advisor, guiding key business decisions and company growth.He is also a CNCF Ambassador, SANS Instructor for SEC540: Cloud Security and DevSecOps Automation, and an active member of CNCF TAG Security.

person.name: Justin Gilley
person.bio: I enjoy the continual process of rethinking technology to produce better software.  Kubernetes is not just a container orchestration tool; Kubernetes is also a philosophy around how to build software for production.  As a person who graduated with a degree in philosophy and later transitioned into tech, I appreciate technologies such as Kubernetes that envision a better future by rethinking the status quo.

person.name: Onur Meraler
person.bio: I’m a DevOps and Cloud Engineer with over three years of experience working with cloud-native technologies including Kubernetes, Azure DevOps, Docker, and Linux.  Currently, I lead Git strategy at KocDigital and specialize in building efficient CI/CD pipelines with a strong focus on containerization, orchestration, infrastructure automation, and monitoring.  I’m passionate about implementing best practices in DevOps and continuously improving the developer experience through automation and observability.  As part of my journey in the cloud-native ecosystem, I’ve been actively working with Kubernetes and related CNCF projects to deliver scalable and reliable solutions.  Always eager to learn, collaborate, and contribute to the community. 🚀

person.name: Umesh Pagar
person.bio: ​I am a proficient DevOps Engineer transitioning my expertise to MLOps and cloud-native solutions. I specialize in managing secure, scalable AI/ML infrastructure and applications  on cloud.

person.name: Oluwasegun John Adebayo
person.bio: Oluwasegun John Adebayo is an experienced Site Reliability Engineer and DevOps specialist with a strong focus on Kubernetes, cloud-native platforms, and observability. He has a proven track record in building, scaling, and securing complex infrastructures in enterprise and regulated environments. His expertise spans DevSecOps, automation, and Infrastructure as Code, enabling efficient and reliable platform operations. John is also known for driving cost optimization, implementing SLO-driven reliability practices, and leading cross-functional engineering initiatives.

person.name: Semjon Nordmann
person.bio: Platform Engineer with expertise in building secure, scalable cloud-native solutions. Passionate about creating developer-friendly tooling that maintains security and compliance standards in containerized applications. Experienced in implementing GitOps workflows, infrastructure-as-code, service mesh architectures, and multi-cluster management strategies.

person.name: Thabelo Ramabulana
person.bio: I'm a self-taught cloud native practitioner with an unconventional background spanning civil engineering studies, subsistence farming, and now specializing in containers as a Cloud Support Engineer at AWS. My three-year journey from farming to DevOps engineering taught me that continuous learning and resilience are key to mastering technologies like Kubernetes, ArgoCD, and Helm. I'm passionate about learning in public and currently launched a YouTube channel focused on DevOps, platform engineering, and CNCF technologies. My unique path from farm to cloud reinforces my belief that cloud native technologies should be accessible to practitioners from all backgrounds.

person.name: Jie-Wei Xie
person.bio: I am an open-source software enthusiast with a strong interest in exploring the CNCF ecosystem. I focus on deepening my understanding of Kubernetes and its surrounding projects through hands-on practice. I enjoy participating in community Meetups to exchange knowledge with others and stay updated on the latest cloud-native developments. I am always keen to learn from the community and grow alongside the evolving open-source landscape.

person.name: Philip Schmid
person.bio: My journey in Information Technology began as a traditional network and Linux system engineer. In 2015, I was introduced to the groundbreaking concept of containers, which sparked a new direction in my career. Over the years, I have honed my skills with various container technologies, including Docker Compose, Docker Swarm, and Kubernetes. By 2020, I decided to fully embrace the future of IT by shifting my focus to Kubernetes, Container Networking, and Cloud Native tooling. This transition has allowed me to dive deep into the evolving landscape of modern infrastructure, where I continue to explore and innovate.

person.name: Robert Rataj
person.bio: I’m a DevOps Engineer focused on cloud-native technologies and Kubernetes. I enjoy building and automating reliable platforms, and I’m always looking for practical ways to make systems simpler, safer, and easier to operate. I’m also a tech enthusiast who loves exploring new tools and sharing ideas with others.

person.name: Danilo Granados
person.bio: I'm a telecommunications professional with expertise in mobile core network engineering. Building upon my core network background, my interest in 5G and Open RAN led me into a parallel path that became what is now a journey into Cloud Native technologies. While leading a team that manages critical voice services in my current role, I've dedicated my personal time to mastering cloud-native concepts and developing a deep understanding of containerized network functions orchestration. As the telecom industry slowly adopts these technologies, my objective is to leverage my extensive telecom background with these recently acquired skills to help drive the industry's inevitable transformation toward Cloud Native architectures.  I've documented key moments of my learning journey and technical insights on my blog: https://medium.com/@danilo.j.granados

person.name: Vyom Yadav
person.bio: Vyom is a final-year computer science student who has been working with open source throughout his degree program. He has been awarded the Google OSPB Award for contributing to Kubernetes. He has been a contributor to Kubernetes, Kyverno and KubeArmor. He co-organizes the CNCF Chandigarh Chapter.
